qa_pairs.question: How do you extract files from all slices of a `dar` archive when using its base name?
qa_pairs.answer: To extract from all slices of a `dar` archive, you can use the `dar -O -x` command followed by the archive's base name, for example: `dar -O -x monday`.

qa_pairs.question: Are there any external scripts available to assist with using `dar`?
qa_pairs.answer: Yes, a team member has written bash functions that can facilitate the use of `dar`.

qa_pairs.question: What is the intended purpose of the external `dar` bash functions?
qa_pairs.answer: Users can use these bash functions as inspiration to write their own `dar` scripts.

qa_pairs.question: Where can users find more information about the external `dar` scripts?
qa_pairs.answer: More details about the external `dar` scripts can be found at `https://github.com/razoumov/sharedSnippets`.
